prompt:
Found this in the gateway logs during yesterday's cache-cold restart:

  09:12:04.101 GET upstream /catalog/config (req 41)
  09:12:04.103 GET upstream /catalog/config (req 42)
  09:12:04.105 GET upstream /catalog/config (req 43)
  ... 38 more identical lines ...
  09:12:09.870 upstream 503 (rate limited)

Every request handler sees an empty cache and independently refetches the same key. Classic stampede. I want a singleflight cache, flightcache.ts, exporting class FlightCache<V>:

- get(key, loader): if a load for that key is already in flight, join it — the later caller's loader must never run. All waiters get the same resolved value (or all get the same rejection).
- Rejections are NOT cached. Every waiter of the failed flight sees the error, then the entry is gone and the next get() tries the loader again.
- Successes are cached. Optional ttlMs in the constructor: a value is fresh strictly less than ttlMs after it was STORED (not requested — loads can be slow), stale at exactly ttlMs. No ttlMs = cache forever. Take an injectable now() -> ms in the constructor too (default Date.now) — that's how the TTL tests stay deterministic.
- invalidate(key): drop the cached value. Subtle case the last homegrown version got wrong: invalidate while a flight is pending means whoever is already waiting still gets the result, but the result must NOT be stored — a write just superseded it. clear() drops everything.

Tests in test_flightcache.ts are the contract: `node --test test_flightcache.ts`. Don't modify them. No dependencies.
